nodes.norm_label: after correction:     - skip rows where every required field is empty.     - w

nodes.norm_label: return the first non-empty value from the row for any of the given keys.

nodes.norm_label: parse a multi-section ims export file.     returns { section_name: [row_dicts,

nodes.label: Missing X-Department-Id header mistake
nodes.rationale: All non-auth frontend requests must include X-Department-Id; axios interceptor in api.ts adds it automatically, raw fetch bypasses it causing 403 errors.

nodes.label: Prisma schema change without migration mistake
nodes.rationale: Schema edits without running 'npx prisma migrate dev' cause runtime column-not-exist errors or Prisma type errors.
nodes.norm_label: prisma schema change without migration mistake

nodes.label: JWT_SECRET mismatch mistake
nodes.rationale: Root .env and backend/.env must share the same JWT_SECRET or all logins return 401 after backend restart.

nodes.label: New protected route missing authMiddleware mistake
nodes.rationale: Every protected route file must import and apply authMiddleware or the route is accessible without login and req.userId is undefined.
nodes.norm_label: new protected route missing authmiddleware mistake

nodes.label: Instantiating Prisma outside utils/prisma.ts mistake
nodes.rationale: Creating 'new PrismaClient' outside utils/prisma.ts causes multiple DB connections and hot-reload connection leaks in dev; should import the shared singleton instead.

nodes.label: Test Generation Framework (Analyze/Strategy/Mock Design/Unit/Integration/Quality Check)
nodes.rationale: Structured six-step approach ensures comprehensive coverage: analyze target structure, choose test strategy, design mocks, write unit tests, write integration tests, then quality-check coverage and naming.

nodes.label: AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)
nodes.rationale: Standard test structuring convention recommended for unit test clarity and consistency.

nodes.label: Merge/finalize floor plans — auto-generate parity with manual
nodes.norm_label: merge/finalize floor plans — auto-generate parity with manual

nodes.label: Merge/Finalize parity problem (auto vs manual buildings)
nodes.rationale: Finalize worked for manual buildings but not auto-generated ones: finalized perimeter lost slanted drawn edges, finalize wiped floor objects, auto floors scattered in preview, cores didn't stack, generated floors landed outside canvas, finalize union shell traced interior zones instead of footprint.
nodes.norm_label: merge/finalize parity problem (auto vs manual buildings)

nodes.label: Floor plan routes lack audit logging follow-up
nodes.rationale: Floor plan routes write no audit entries, so overwrites are unrecoverable; consider audit logging with oldValues.
nodes.norm_label: floor plan routes lack audit logging follow-up

nodes.label: Auth flow (login → JWT → localStorage → headers → authMiddleware)
nodes.norm_label: auth flow (login → jwt → localstorage → headers → authmiddleware)

nodes.label: rec-api-axios-only recommendation
nodes.rationale: Shared axios instance automatically injects Authorization and X-Department-Id headers; bypassing it produces unauthenticated or department-unscoped requests causing 401/403 errors.

nodes.label: rec-auth-department-scope recommendation
nodes.rationale: Backend department isolation relies on middleware order; routes without authMiddleware are publicly accessible, routes without a scope guard can leak cross-department data.

nodes.label: rec-raw-fetch-cleanup recommendation
nodes.rationale: Several pages use raw fetch instead of services/api.ts, violating the frontend rule; not currently blocking since affected endpoints only require authMiddleware (no department-scope guard), but should be migrated for consistency and future safety.

nodes.label: Auth middleware order convention (authMiddleware -> scope guard -> handler)
nodes.rationale: Establishes a fixed middleware order so every protected route enforces authentication then department scope before reaching business logic.
nodes.norm_label: auth middleware order convention (authmiddleware -> scope guard -> handler)

nodes.label: Release verification checklist (8 steps)
nodes.rationale: Defines pre-merge gates: schema migration status, env var coverage, route guard presence, no raw fetch/axios.create, clean build, secrets scan, changelog, rollback plan — to ensure safe releases.

nodes.label: CI Backend Build and Test Job
nodes.norm_label: ci backend build and test job

nodes.label: Session Start Protocol
nodes.rationale: Mandates loading COMMON_MISTAKES.md, QUICK_START.md, and ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md at session start (~800 tokens) so agents avoid known pitfalls cheaply; explicitly excludes completions/sessions/archive docs from auto-load to keep token cost at zero for historical records.

nodes.label: Context / Compact Rule
nodes.rationale: Standard context target is 200K tokens (CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW); auto-compact triggers at ~75% usage (CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE=75) leaving ~50K safety space, because compacting at 100K (50%) was found too early for long IMS sessions. Manual /compact is not percentage-based and compresses full history.

nodes.label: Multi-Floor Auto Generation
nodes.rationale: Original auto-generation created independent plans with no concept of a multi-floor building; added building/floor controls so Floor 1 defines a shared outdoor-wall shape reused by sibling floors, each with its own template.

nodes.label: Stairs, Elevators, and Restrooms Generation
nodes.rationale: Mandatory vertical-access and restroom generation added so every floor is realistic; wording changed from bathroom to restroom; male/female pairs sized equally.

nodes.label: Indoor and Outdoor Object Groups
nodes.rationale: Grouping indoor walls by room and outdoor walls as one building shell makes generated layouts easier to move and regenerate while staying contained by the outdoor footprint.

nodes.label: Randomized Indoor Layout Placement
nodes.rationale: Expanded regeneration randomization beyond small offsets so indoor room groups can move substantially while staying inside outdoor walls, enabling varied randomized arrangements within the same shell.

nodes.label: Realistic Wall Snapping and Doors
nodes.rationale: Indoor walls near outdoor walls snap/merge with the boundary to avoid duplicate wall edges; doors are relocated off snapped edges to remain functionally placed; spacing reduced to practical values.

nodes.label: Multi-Floor Regeneration
nodes.rationale: Regeneration moved to building level: only the parent Floor 1 plan can trigger regeneration, which cascades to all sibling floors; backend rejects direct sibling-floor regeneration to keep the shared footprint consistent.

nodes.label: Optional Outdoor-Wall Regeneration
nodes.rationale: Added a 'Regenerate outdoor walls' toggle so users can preserve existing outdoor walls exactly and randomize only indoor layouts, giving more control with progress/validation feedback.

nodes.label: Overflow and Floor Suggestions
nodes.rationale: Capacity-aware location assignment ensures locations are assigned once and not duplicated across floors; Auto Generate suggests additional floors based on remaining location types and unused capacity, informed by office/storage/dormitory/factory/warehouse/shelf/rack planning guidance.

nodes.label: Sibling-Floor Fit Correction
nodes.rationale: Fixed a bug where Floor 2/3 indoor layouts appeared extremely small due to uniform scaling against the Floor 1 outdoor-wall bounding box; replaced with placement search inside the actual outdoor-wall polygon and capacity-aware floor suggestion.

nodes.label: Paired Stair Logic
nodes.rationale: Added 'Pair stairs by floors' option so stairs align in floor pairs (1-2, 3-4, 5-6) for realistic vertical access, vs. reusing Floor 1's stair location on every floor when disabled; applies to both generation and building regeneration.

nodes.label: Rooftop Floor Option
nodes.rationale: Added 'Add rooftop floor' option producing a location-free rooftop sharing the outdoor-wall footprint; when occupied-floor count is odd, the last occupied floor pairs stairs with the rooftop, keeping pairing rules consistent.

nodes.label: Fixed Stair Size (2m x 2m)
nodes.rationale: Stairs fixed at 2.00m x 2.00m so fitting logic no longer scales the stair group, keeping the complete stair group (room, walls, door) physically realistic and consistent.

nodes.label: Shared Elevator Shaft
nodes.rationale: Elevators use a fixed 2.00m x 2.00m shaft with Floor 1 defining its location; every sibling floor and rooftop reuses the exact same shaft location for physical correctness, independent of stair-pairing behavior.

nodes.label: Removed Sibling Object Resizing
nodes.rationale: Removed automatic resizing of sibling-floor indoor objects so they retain originally generated width/height; the fitting helper may reposition but not resize the indoor layout, and unchanged-size layouts that cannot fit retain their original geometry.

nodes.label: Repository Branch Policy
nodes.rationale: main is the stable client-facing release branch, develop is client-facing development, staging is internal validation; GitHub cannot make a single branch private in a public repo, so a private staging branch requires a private repo or fork instead.

nodes.label: Version Tag Convention
nodes.rationale: Tags like v1.0.develop, v1.1.develop mark reviewed app changes, bug fixes, and issue-fix milestones on the develop branch.

nodes.label: Vulnerability Reporting Process
nodes.rationale: Vulnerabilities must be reported privately (email or GitHub Security Advisory), never as a public issue, to avoid exposing exploit details before a fix ships; response within 7 days.
